Ashes 2025/26: Australia vs England – 1st Test Preview

Nov 20, 2025

Ashes 2025/26: Australia vs England – 1st Test Preview

The Ashes return Down Under, and the excitement is already peaking.
England head into Perth looking to end their long drought on Australian soil, while Australia must defend home advantage with a slightly reshuffled squad.
Here’s a complete breakdown of the numbers, matchups, and trends shaping the series opener.

Historical Context

  • Perth Stadium Dominance: Australia has won 8 of their last 10 Tests at this venue. England’s only recent win came during the 2010/11 Ashes.
  • Series Openers in Australia (Post-WWII): England have won only 2 of 20 opening Tests played in Australia.
  • Team Stability: England’s consistency under Bazball could be an advantage, especially with Australia missing frontline pacers Pat Cummins and Josh Hazlewood.

Team Lineups and Key Notes

Australia

Strengths: Strong home record, experienced top order
Weaknesses: Missing key pacers
Key Players: Smith, Khawaja, Warner
Updates: Brendan Doggett set to debut; Jake Weatherald likely to open

England

Strengths: Aggressive batting, settled XI
Weaknesses: Historically struggle in Australian series openers
Key Players: Root, Stokes, Archer, Wood
Updates: Archer returning from injury; Wood fully fit

Batting Trends (Top 7 Comparison)

Team Runs Avg SR 100s 50s Dot% Att% Def% No%
Australia 2377 34.95 51.4 2 4 37% 29.2% 37.9% 19%
England 2536 41.57 75.3 1 6 59.5% 43.4% 28.7% 8.8%

Bazball Impact: England’s aggressive approach has significantly boosted their strike rates and run accumulation, especially overseas.
Australia’s weakened pace attack may be tested early.

Opening Partnerships

Team Innings Avg Century Stands Highest
Australia 29 40.34 3 385
England 29 21.58 0 55

England will rely on Crawley–Duckett, their most successful pair in recent years.
Khawaja’s dip in form (31.28 avg since 2024) remains a concern for Australia.

Key Player Matchups

Player Bowler Inns Runs Wkts Avg SR Notes
Smith Wood 16 128 4 32 51.8 England pace challenge
Smith Archer 5 94 0 NA 57.3 Requires aggression
Root Cummins 31 286 11 26 53.4 Vulnerable outside off
Root Hazlewood 31 314 10 31 52.3 Consistency issues
Khawaja Wood 8 72 3 24 45.3 Weak vs round-the-wicket
Stokes Lyon 43 134 7 19 63 Dominant vs off-spin

Factors Likely to Decide the Perth Test

  • Pace Battle: England’s fit and fiery pace lineup could trouble Australia’s new-look attack.
  • Opening Partnerships: Strong starts will be crucial.
  • Bazball Tempo: England’s attacking approach may pressure inexperienced bowlers.
  • Key Performers: Smith, Root, Stokes, Crawley, and Duckett expected to dictate the contest.
  • Pitch Behavior: Perth traditionally offers pace and bounce — ideal for fast bowlers and attacking batting.
